channel.title: NYT > Arts > Music
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: How Did Flea Make a Jazz Album? Practice, Practice, Practice.
item.description: The Red Hot Chili Peppers bassist returned to the trumpet, for a new record featuring Nick Cave, Thom Yorke and a core cast of contemporary jazz luminaries.
item.dc:creator: Hank Shteamer and Ariel Fisher
item.media:credit: Ariel Fisher for The New York Times

item.title: As BTS Returns From the Military, There’s a Precedent: Elvis
item.description: With the K-pop group releasing its comeback album, “Arirang,” on Friday, look back at how one of pop’s original kings handled his time out of the spotlight.
item.dc:creator: Gavin Edwards
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Chung Sung-Jun/Getty Images
item.media:description: From left, RM and V of BTS saluted after being discharged from their military service in June.

item.title: With Twin Babies, the Opera Star Lise Davidsen Wonders What Comes Next
item.description: Davidsen, who has returned triumphantly to the Met in “Tristan und Isolde,” said that singing “has been my world, my everything. And now it’s not.”
item.dc:creator: Zachary Woolfe
item.media:credit: Amir Hamja for The New York Times

item.title: The Loud Nights and Quiet Days of Avalon Emerson
item.description: Known as a D.J. at one of Berlin’s most storied clubs, she swerved with an LP of dreamy pop. Now she’s back, cataloging the angst of 30-something creative life.
item.dc:creator: Foster Kamer and Tim Barber
item.media:credit: Tim Barber for The New York Times

item.title: Monopoly? Not a Chance, Says Live Nation’s Chief at Antitrust Trial.
item.description: Michael Rapino, the chief executive of the live entertainment colossus, fought back against accusations his company unfairly dominates the music industry.
item.dc:creator: Ben Sisario
item.media:credit: Michael M. Santiago/Getty Images
item.media:description: Michael Rapino, the chief executive of Live Nation, defended the company from accusations that it is a monopoly that does not care about its customers.

item.title: Review: ‘The Wild Party’ Has a Ball at City Center
item.description: Encores! revisits a Jazz Age tale of debauchery, with showstoppers from Jasmine Amy Rogers, Adrienne Warren, Jordan Donica, Tonya Pinkins and others.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw
item.category: Encores! Great American Musicals in Concert (Series)
item.media:credit: Hiroko Masuike/The New York Times
item.media:description: Adrienne Warren as Kate, left, and Jasmine Amy Rogers as Queenie in the Encores! staging of “The Wild Party” at New York City Center in Manhattan.

item.title: Brooklyn Academy of Music Names New President: Tamara McCaw
item.description: Tamara McCaw, a longtime arts leader with experience at the Brooklyn Academy of Music, will lead it with a focus on stability.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nagourney
item.media:credit: Brooklyn Academy of Music
item.media:description: Tamara McCaw, the new president of the Brooklyn Academy of Music.

item.title: 2 Chainz, One of Hip-Hop’s Cheekiest Lyricists, Gets Serious
item.description: The rapper known for his quirky turns of phrase and malapropisms is trying his hand at a memoir.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Abrams
item.category: The Voice in My Head Is God (Book)
item.media:credit: Alexander J. Rotondo for The New York Times

item.title: Afroman Wins Civil Trial Over Use of Police Raid Footage in His Music Videos
item.description: Seven law enforcement officers had accused the rapper, whose name is Joseph E. Foreman, of causing them “humiliation” by using images from their 2022 raid on his Ohio home in two music videos.
item.dc:creator: Livia Albeck-Ripka
item.media:credit: Johnny Louis/Getty Images
item.media:description: The rapper Afroman, whose real name is Joseph E. Foreman, performing in Hollywood, Fla., in 2018.

item.title: ‘My Joy Is Heavy’ Review: Hope and Horror Live in the Same House
item.description: A full-scale production of the Bengsons’ deeply personal memoir musical is delivered via anthemic songs and remnants of home.
item.dc:creator: Brittani Samuel

item.title: Goo Goo Dolls’ ‘Iris’ Is Back as ’90s Trend Soundtrack
item.description: Courteney Cox, basketball fans and Marvel movies have recently joined in on the resurgence of the band’s signature anthemic ballad.
item.dc:creator: Sopan Deb
item.media:credit: Nina Westervelt for The New York Times
item.media:description: John Rzeznik performing with the Goo Goo Dolls in New York City this month. “Iris,” the band’s anthemic 1998 ballad, has been used as the soundtrack to a recent nostalgic TikTok trend.

item.title: BTS: A Guide to the K-Pop Group’s Discography
item.description: As the boy band returns on Friday with “Arirang,” listen to key albums and solo releases by its seven members.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Benjamin

item.title: 8 Great Songs That Make It Easy to Be Green
item.description: Celebrate St. Patrick’s Day with Ray Charles’s take on Kermit the Frog’s classic and tracks from Lorde, Booker T. & the M.G.’s, Joni Mitchell and more.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Zoladz
item.media:credit: CBS/Getty Images
item.media:description: Kermit the Frog and Ray Charles

item.title: Grace Ives Was Tipped as a Top Indie-Rock Star. Then She Hit the Bottom.
item.description: Her 2022 breakthrough, “Janky Star,” won her accolades and devotees, but she didn’t begin a follow-up until she got sober and started fresh.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Zoladz and Caroline Tompkins
item.media:credit: Caroline Tompkins for The New York Times

item.title: Inside the Katseye Fandom: Friendships, Rivalries and Boycotts Over Manon’s Hiatus
item.description: Fans of the group have leveraged their influence into friendship and rivalry. Then a member’s sudden hiatus sent them into a spiral.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai and Matt Yan
item.media:credit: Alexander Coggin for The New York Times
item.media:description: “You have to remind yourself that you don’t know them personally,” said Joy Lebofsky, who has experienced the joys and disappointments of being a stan.

item.title: Live Nation Antitrust Case Resumes With Testimony About Springsteen Fees
item.description: After the Justice Department announced a deal with the concert giant, the trial picked up after a week’s break, with a coalition of states leading the way.
item.dc:creator: Ben Sisario
item.media:credit: Mario Tama/Getty Images
item.media:description: In testimony on Monday, the chief executive of AEG Presents spoke about competing against Live Nation.

item.title: Oscars Cut Off ‘KPop Demon Hunters’ Speech in Brutal Live Moment
item.description: After the hit song from “KPop Demon Hunters” made Oscars history, a snare drum and a crashing cymbal mercilessly drowned out the winners.
item.dc:creator: Derrick Bryson Taylor
item.media:credit: Chris Pizzello/Invision, via Associated Press

item.title: Kennedy Center Board Votes to Close for 2-Year Renovation Project
item.description: Ahead of the vote, President Trump criticized the center’s physical condition and previous financial management.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs
item.category: Kennedy, John F, Center for the Performing Arts
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: “What I know best in the world is construction,” President Trump said at a news conference about planned renovations at the John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts.

item.title: Review: ‘The Silent Serenade’ at Mannes Opera
item.description: Erich Wolfgang Korngold’s “The Silent Serenade,” written in the 1940s, was given its English-language, United States premiere by Mannes Opera.
item.dc:creator: Seth Colter Walls

item.title: Review: Handel’s ‘Hercules’ at Carnegie Hall
item.description: At Carnegie Hall, the English Concert made a case for “Hercules” as a strikingly modern dramatic oratorio of psychological and musical depth.
item.dc:creator: Corinna da Fonseca-Wollheim

item.title: Kacey Musgraves, the Pussycat Dolls: 8 Songs We’re Talking About This Week
item.description: Kacey Musgraves laments her frustration, the Pussycat Dolls attempt a comeback and Bella Kay cracks the Top 40 with a tune about turmoil.
item.dc:creator: Jon Pareles
item.media:credit: Seth Herald/Reuters
item.media:description: Kacey Musgraves announced a new album, “Middle of Nowhere,” with a song filled with not-so-subtle references.

item.title: Gorillaz on ‘The Mountain’ and Celebrating Lost Musicians
item.description: “We need young artists to engage with other things than fame and image and likes,” Damon Albarn said. The band’s latest album proves the point.
item.dc:creator: Brian Raftery
item.media:credit: OK McCausland for The New York Times
item.media:description: Jamie Hewlett and Damon Albarn’s latest Gorillaz album features voices of lost former collaborators.

item.title: Coalition of States Will Carry on Live Nation Antitrust Case
item.description: The federal government has settled its claims, but dozens of states on Monday will resume a trial that accuses the company of being a monopoly.
item.dc:creator: Ben Sisario
item.media:credit: Patrick T. Fallon/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Live Nation reached a settlement with the Justice Department that did not satisfy the attorneys general of many states.

item.title: Crisis Follows Conductor’s Dismissal at the Boston Symphony
item.description: A decision by the orchestra’s board to fire its music director, Andris Nelsons, has set off protests from players, leaving their leaders scrambling.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nagourney

item.title: Kneecap Gets Serious on Its New Album, ‘Fenian’
item.description: The gonzo Irish M.C.s first gained notice for their playful antics. After attracting global attention for their pro-Palestinian views, their new LP is more serious.
item.dc:creator: Alex Marshall and Max Miechowski
item.media:credit: Max Miechowski for The New York Times

item.title: Jack Harlow Was a Chart-Topping Rapper. He Doesn’t Want to Brag Anymore.
item.description: After smashes like “First Class” and “Lovin on Me,” the artist from Louisville, Ky., is making a true musical shift to intimate, hand-played R&B on his fourth album, “Monica.”
item.dc:creator: Joe Coscarelli, Jon Caramanica and Lucia Bell-Epstein For The New York Times
item.media:credit: Lucia Bell-Epstein for The New York Times

item.title: 1,865 Coats in 15 Minutes: A Fine-Tuned Opera House Team
item.description: Each night, the coat check at the Deutsche Oper in Berlin deals with hundreds of jackets, no matter how fancy the audience dresses.
item.dc:creator: Christopher F. Schuetze and Gordon Welters
item.media:credit: Gordon Welters for The New York Times
